definition: Loop mass flow rate solved from buoyancy versus modeled hydraulic loss
notes: Primary hydraulic state

definition: Bulk temperature at each control-volume boundary around the loop
notes: One node per segment boundary

definition: Density evaluated at segment mean bulk temperature
notes: Use explicit salt material branch

definition: Dynamic viscosity evaluated at segment mean bulk temperature
notes: Needed for Reynolds number

definition: Specific heat evaluated at segment mean bulk temperature
notes: Used in energy march

definition: Thermal conductivity evaluated at segment mean bulk temperature
notes: Needed for optional Nu to HTC path

definition: Effective Reynolds number from mdot geometry and viscosity
notes: Primary hydraulic and thermal fit input

definition: Effective Prandtl number from material properties
notes: Used only where direct Nu mode is enabled

definition: Integrated buoyancy head from density and elevation map
notes: Drives natural circulation

definition: Apparent Darcy friction factor
notes: Defended only on lower_leg and test_section_span

definition: Straight-section pressure loss from defended friction fit
notes: Use only on admitted straight classes

definition: Effective thermal conductance per unit length
notes: Primary thermal surface

definition: Effective heat transfer coefficient
notes: Secondary to UA' in v1

definition: Effective Nusselt number
notes: Fitted direct use only on left_lower_leg

definition: Imposed heater power on heater segments
notes: Visible readable control

definition: Imposed cooler sink on cooler bucket
notes: Use fixed negative Q not live cooler h

definition: Lumped unresolved thermal loss remainder
notes: Needed until full branch thermal coverage expands

definition: Lumped unresolved hydraulic loss remainder
notes: Absorbs blocked feature and return-path losses in v1

definition: Ambient temperature used in external loss terms
notes: Case operating input

definition: Arbitrary pressure reference for reporting only
notes: Not needed for loop closure if only pressure differences are used
